comment: C-029 calibration for the breach proposer (breach/proposers/propose.js). The reconciliation gate in the old estate checked SITE reachability, not per-rule PAGE coverage, so absence rules fired on pages nobody read (coverage-contract as reporting theatre instead of blocking data). Here the required complaints-procedure disclosure is genuinely absent from the crawl, which WOULD yield an absence-breach if the complaints page-class were covered - but the crawl read only the homepage, so coverage-contract.coverageFor() marks the rule 'screened'. propose() must therefore emit NO FIRING candidate for FAKE_ACT_2099_SCREENED_COMPLAINTS: a screened rule never proposes. A recorded suppression entry (suppressed_reason set, artifact null) is the correct, visible abstention (C-037) and is not a fired candidate. No real law text: FAKE_ACT_2099 id only (C-071). The record is nested under 'catalogue' (never a top-level 'records' array) so the catalogue linters' glob-load correctly skips this non-pack fixture.

website_obligations.duty: Publish the firm complaints procedure and how to escalate a complaint to the ombudsman
elements: complaints procedure findable on the website

pages.title: Home
pages.text: Welcome to our firm. We are a friendly team helping clients across the region every day with real dedication.

pages.title: About
pages.text: About our firm. We were founded in the year two thousand and one and take pride in careful, honest work.

pages.title: Services
pages.text: Our services span many areas of practice and we always aim to help every client we possibly can here.

corpus.footerText: Contact us at the front desk during opening hours.

expect.note: coverage is computed by the test via evidence/crawler/coverage-contract.coverageFor(records, pages); the complaints page-class is never crawled, so the rule is screened and propose() yields no fired candidate.
